next_action: step2_mask_sanity gate PASSED with real floor/treatment/oracle numbers (see gates.step2_mask_sanity). Next: decide whether to proceed to step3 (Fourier-only sanity) per CLAUDE.md build order, or dig into why mixture underperforms isolated mask before moving on -- human input requested via pi-updates.md draft, not yet sent.

gates.step0_smoke: PASS - all 4 corruption modes run without crash, finite loss, on real IN-1K
gates.step2_mask_sanity: PASS (2026-07-06) - masked-recovery eval, mask_prob=0.5, n=256 held-out IN-1K val images, seed=0 (RNG-matched across arms). mean_masked_mse: gaussian floor=0.24237, mask treatment=0.11313, mixture treatment=0.14025, VAE-roundtrip oracle=0.01488. normalized_gap = (floor-treatment)/(floor-oracle): mask=0.568, mixture=0.449. Mask-trained model recovers masked regions ~2.1x better than gaussian-trained model (57% of the floor-to-oracle headroom closed) -- masking the training start-state teaches the model to use visible context, which pure-Gaussian training never sees. Mixture arm captures less of the gain (45%) than isolated mask (57%), consistent with dilution across 3 arms (gaussian+mask+fourier weight 1,1,1 each) rather than a stronger combined effect -- worth flagging in PI update, not yet interpreted further pending step3 (Fourier-only) isolated result. Loss-curve comparison from earlier (mask ~5.2 vs gaussian ~11.2) is NOT part of this verdict -- retracted separately as an easiness artifact of the training target, unrelated to this outcome-metric-based PASS.
